questions.q: A and B are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio of 3:2. A retires from the firm. The goodwill of the firm immediately after retirement is Rs 70,000. Calculate the amount of goodwill attributable to A.
State the journal entry to be recorded when a partner retires and is to receive a share of profit till the date of retirement in proportion to the capital.
questions.answer: Debit Partner's Capital Account and Credit Profit and Loss A/c

questions.q: A and B are partners in the ratio of 5:3. C joins the firm with 1/8th share. The new profit-sharing ratio is 4:3:2. Calculate the amount of goodwill attributable to C.
When a partner dies, what is the treatment of his share of goodwill?
questions.answer: It is not distributed among the remaining partners.

questions.q: On the death of a partner, the following assets and liabilities are transferred to the deceased partner's estate. Depreciated machinery Rs 80,000, Cash Rs 10,000, Debtors Rs 20,000, Creditors Rs 15,000. What is the value of the estate's share?
A and B are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio of 3:2. A retires from the firm and receives Rs 24,000 as retirement compensation. What is the amount of goodwill attributable to A?
True or False: When a partner retires, the goodwill of the firm is not affected.
A and B are partners in the ratio of 3:2. C joins the firm with 1/4th share. The new profit-sharing ratio is 2:1:1. Calculate the amount of goodwill attributable to B.

A and B are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio of 4:3. A dies and his share of goodwill is adjusted by transferring a sum of Rs 6,000 to the deceased partner's estate. What is the value of the estate's share?
State the journal entry to be recorded when a partner retires and is to receive a share of profit till the date of retirement in proportion to the capital and also a share of goodwill.
questions.answer: Debit Partner's Capital Account and Credit Profit and Loss A/c, and Goodwill A/c
